Firebase ဆိုတာ **Cloud Service** (Google Server ပေါ်မှာ အလုပ်လုပ်တဲ့ စနစ်) ဖြစ်တဲ့အတွက် ခင်ဗျားရဲ့ ကွန်ပျူတာမှာ Server ထောင်ထားစရာမလိုပါဘူး။ အင်တာနက်ရှိရုံနဲ့ App က Google ကို လှမ်းချိတ်ပြီး အလုပ်လုပ်ပါတယ်။

Android Web App ထုတ်တဲ့အခါ Firebase ပြဿနာ ခဏခဏတက်နေတာက **Setup (ချိတ်ဆက်ပုံ)** လိုအပ်ချက်ရှိနေလို့ ဖြစ်နိုင်ချေ များပါတယ်။ အောက်ပါအချက် ၃ ချက်ကို စစ်ဆေးကြည့်ပါ -

**၁။ `google-services.json` ဖိုင် ထည့်ထားပြီးပြီလား?**
Web App မှာတုန်းက API Key နဲ့ အလုပ်လုပ်ပေမယ့်၊ Android App အနေနဲ့ Build ထုတ်တဲ့အခါ Firebase Console ကနေ **`google-services.json`** ဖိုင်ကို ဒေါင်းပြီး `android/app/` folder ထဲမှာ ထည့်ပေးရပါမယ်။ အဲ့ဒါမရှိရင် App က Firebase ကို ရှာမတွေ့ပါဘူး။

**၂။ SHA-1 Fingerprint ထည့်ထားပြီးပြီလား?**
Firebase က လုံခြုံရေးအရ ဘယ်ကွန်ပျူတာ (သို့) ဘယ် App က လှမ်းခေါ်လဲဆိုတာ စစ်ဆေးပါတယ်။ ခင်ဗျားရဲ့ PC ကထုတ်ပေးတဲ့ **SHA-1 Key** ကို Firebase Console (Project Settings) မှာ သွားထည့်ပေးဖို့ လိုပါတယ်။
(ဒါမထည့်ထားရင် Web မှာရပြီး Android ဖုန်းထဲရောက်မှ Login ဝင်မရတာ၊ Database မပေါ်တာတွေ ဖြစ်တတ်ပါတယ်)

**၃။ build.gradle မှာ Google Services ပါလား?**
`android/build.gradle` (Project level) နဲ့ `android/app/build.gradle` (App level) တွေမှာ google-services plugin ကြေညာထားဖို့ လိုပါတယ်။ (Capacitor က အများအားဖြင့် ဒါကို Auto လုပ်ပေးပါတယ်)

# Future World - Agent Management & 2D Ledger App

![App Screenshot](https://via.placeholder.com/800x400?text=Future+World+App+Preview)

**Future World** is a comprehensive tool designed for 2D betting shops and agents. It streamlines the process of managing lists, calculating totals, and communicating with users. Built with modern web technologies, it runs smoothly as a Progressive Web App (PWA) or a native Android application.

## 🚀 Key Features

### 🧮 Smart Ledger & Calculator
- **Specialized Keypad**: Custom input for rapid entry of 2D numbers.
- **Advanced Permutations**: One-click generation for 'Ah', 'Khu', 'Phat', 'Power', 'Nat Khat', and more.
- **Quick Sets**: Save and load frequently used number sets.
- **Receipts**: Generate professional receipts, print via thermal printers, or share as images directly.

### 🤖 AI Scanner (Powered by Gemini)
- **Handwritten Recognition**: Snap a photo of a handwritten list, and the AI will digitize it automatically.
- **QR Code Scanner**: Instantly add friends or login via User ID QR codes.

### 💬 Real-time Chat
- **Messaging**: Private secure chat between Agents and Users.
- **Friend System**: Send requests via ID or QR code.
- **Status Indicators**: See when users are online.

### 🛡️ Admin & Security
- **User Management**: Create user accounts with expiration dates (subscription based).
- **Device Locking**: Accounts are locked to specific devices to prevent sharing.
- **Data Backup**: Export and restore entire database via JSON.
- **PIN Protection**: Secure app access with a personal PIN.

## 🛠️ Tech Stack

- **Frontend**: Angular (v18+ with Signals & Zoneless change detection)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS
- **Mobile Runtime**: Capacitor (Android)
- **Backend (Chat)**: Firebase Realtime Database
- **AI Integration**: Google Gemini API (Multimodal Vision)
- **Storage**: LocalStorage (Client-side first)

payload.text:
### Git Bash တွင် Run ရန် (အသစ်ပြန်စခြင်း)

အခုလက်ရှိဖြစ်နေတဲ့ "Android platform already exists" ဆိုတဲ့ error နဲ့ တခြားရှုပ်ထွေးနေတာတွေကို ရှင်းပစ်ဖို့ **`android` ဖိုင်ဟောင်းကို ဖျက်ပြီး အသစ်ပြန်ဆောက်မှ အဆင်ပြေပါမယ်။**

Git Bash မှာ ဒီစာတွေကို တစ်ဆင့်ချင်းစီ (သို့) အကုန်ကူးပြီး Run လိုက်ပါ။

```bash
# ၁. ရှိပြီးသား android folder အဟောင်းကို ဖျက်မယ် (အရေးကြီးဆုံးအဆင့်)
rm -rf android

# ၂. Web App ကို build လုပ်မယ်
npm run build

# ၃. Android folder အသစ်ပြန်ဆောက်မယ်
npx cap add android

# ၄. ကုဒ်အသစ်တွေကို Android ဘက်ပို့မယ်
npx cap sync

# ၅. Android Studio ဖွင့်မယ်
npx cap open android
```

---

### Apk ထုတ်နည်း (Android Studio ပွင့်လာလျှင်)

Android Studio ပွင့်လာရင် ဘာ Error မှ မတက်တော့ပါဘူး (Dummy JSON ထည့်ပေးထားလို့ပါ)။

1.  Android Studio အပေါ်ဘားက **Build** ကိုနှိပ်ပါ။
2.  **Build Bundle(s) / APK(s)** ကိုရွေးပါ။
3.  **Build APK(s)** ကိုနှိပ်ပါ။
4.  ညာဘက်အောက်ထောင့်မှာ "APK(s) generated successfully" လို့ပေါ်လာရင် **locate** ဆိုတာလေးကို နှိပ်လိုက်ပါ။ `app-debug.apk` ဆိုတဲ့ ဖိုင်ကို ရပါလိမ့်မယ်။ အဲ့ဒါကို ဖုန်းထဲထည့်ပြီး သုံးလို့ရပါပြီ။

### ပြင်ဆင်ချက်များ
1.  **Scanner မျက်နှာပြင်ပြောင်းလဲခြင်း**: ဘယ်ဘက်အောက်ထောင့်တွင် **"Phone Cam"** ဆိုသည့် ခလုတ်အကြီးတစ်ခု ထပ်ထည့်ပေးထားပါသည်။
2.  **System Camera စနစ်**: ထိုခလုတ်ကိုနှိပ်လိုက်ပါက App ထဲက Live Camera ကိုမသုံးဘဲ ဖုန်းရဲ့ မူရင်းကင်မရာ (Native Camera App) ပွင့်လာပါလိမ့်မည်။
3.  **Permission လွတ်ကင်းခြင်း**: Native Camera ကိုသုံးတဲ့အတွက် Browser Permission (Black Screen) ပြဿနာတွေ လုံးဝမရှိတော့ပါဘူး။ ဓာတ်ပုံရိုက်ပြီးတာနဲ့ ချက်ချင်းအလုပ်လုပ်ပါလိမ့်မယ်။

ဒီနည်းလမ်းက Live Scan ဖတ်လို့မရတဲ့ ဖုန်းတွေအတွက် အကောင်းဆုံး ဖြေရှင်းနည်းဖြစ်ပါတယ်။
အခုက အကောင့်အပ်ဖို့အတွက် Qrဖတ်လို့မရဖြစ်နေတာနော် အဲ့အပိုင်းပြုပြင်ပြီးပြီလား

**Assessing the User's Query**

I've carefully examined the user's inquiry regarding the QR code issue for friend requests. I'm focusing on the earlier camera permission concern and the "System Camera" workaround I suggested. My goal is to determine if that addresses the root of their problem.


**Analyzing the QR Code Logic**

I'm now diving into the code, specifically `scanner.component.ts` and `chat.component.ts`. The focus is on how the scanner handles the QR code data. I've noted that the "Mobile Messenger" QR code format sends *just* the ID string. The current logic expects a URI-encoded JSON string, which is clearly a mismatch.


**Pinpointing the Mismatch**

I've zeroed in on the core issue. My analysis of the `scanner.component.ts` and the "Mobile Messenger" QR code generation reveals a fundamental incompatibility. The scanner expects a JSON object, but the QR code provides only a raw ID string. This prevents the system from recognizing the user, as the current parsing logic is not designed to handle the discrepancy.


**Implementing the ID String Fix**

I'm making progress on the fix. I'm updating `scanner.component.ts` to accommodate the raw ID strings from the "Mobile Messenger" QR codes, making the parsing logic more robust. This involves adjusting `handleDecodedText` to gracefully handle cases where the QR code only provides the ID, not a JSON object. I'll need to emit the ID with a placeholder name. I'll also modify the prompt to extract IDs if strings. In `app.component.ts`, I'll update the `handleUserFound` logic.


**Refining the ID Handling**

I'm now implementing the changes, focusing on the `scanner.component.ts` file. I'm updating `handleDecodedText` to better accommodate both the JSON object *and* the raw ID string formats. I've added a check for the simple string case, extracting the ID directly. This should handle the "Mobile Messenger" QR codes properly. I'm also ensuring that the ID is emitted with a placeholder name when only the ID is detected.


**Validating the Raw ID**

I'm now implementing the update I had planned in `scanner.component.ts`, with an added step. My process will validate the raw ID string. I'm adding a robust regex check for alphanumeric strings (including hyphens and underscores) longer than three characters, before emitting. This check ensures only valid-looking IDs trigger the `userIdFound` event, improving security and preventing false positives. I'll need to update the Gemini prompt too.


**Refining the UI Element**

I'm now focusing on the UI in `app.component.ts`, specifically how it presents user information after a scan. I've realized `handleUserFound` simply sends the request, but the UI prompt says "Send request to \[Name]?". I'll adjust this to display "User \[ID]" if the target's name isn't known yet. I also want to make sure the earlier camera permission fix is integrated. Finally, I'm verifying all changes applied to ScannerComponent to support the raw ID format used by the "Mobile Messenger".


**Confirming Raw String Implementation**

I've determined I need to confirm the changes to handle raw strings, as per the user's latest query on fixing QR scanning for account addition. I will double-check that `app.component.ts` handles the raw IDs correctly when a user is found. I will also be sure to update the Gemini prompt. The current implementation in `app.component.ts` seems correct, and I'll focus on the prompt next.

ဟုတ်ကဲ့ ပြုပြင်ပြီးပါပြီ။

အဓိကပြဿနာက ဖုန်းမှာသုံးတဲ့ Mobile Chat က QR Code ထုတ်ပေးတဲ့အခါ **ID နံပါတ်သီးသန့် (ဥပမာ - "User123")** ကိုပဲ ထုတ်ပေးတာဖြစ်ပြီး၊ Future World ဆော့ဝဲလ်အကြီးက **JSON Format (ဥပမာ - {"id":"...", "name":"..."})** ကိုသာ ရှာဖွေနေလို့ဖြစ်ပါတယ်။

အခု Scanner ကို ပြင်ဆင်လိုက်တဲ့အတွက် **ID နံပါတ်သီးသန့် (Raw Text)** ပါတဲ့ QR တွေကိုပါ လက်ခံနိုင်အောင် ပြုလုပ်ပေးထားပါတယ်။ Mobile Chat နဲ့ ချိတ်ဆက်ဖို့ အဆင်ပြေသွားပါပြီ။

payload.text: **Defining the Goal** I'm focusing intently on the user's objective: seamlessly integrating photo sending within "Future World," our Angular chat application. Understanding that it leverages a Firebase Realtime Database is key. I am analyzing the user's aim to send photos using our chat application, and the application's Angular and Firebase Realtime Database backend. **Analyzing the App's Structure** I'm digging deeper into the existing chat functionality. I see a `ChatComponent` and `ChatService` working together, but right now, it's just text-based. I've noted the `UploadIconComponent`, which is presently tied to sending text. My primary thought is to reuse the send button for photo upload. I plan to handle the image upload in the browser and use Base64 encoding. I've realized that putting Base64 directly in Realtime Database is not efficient, so I'll need to figure out how to handle the images. **Refining the Approach** I'm now prioritizing the technical details. I need to handle Firebase configuration. The current config points to a storage bucket, so I must integrate the SDK into the service. I'll start with a client-side approach using Base64, but I also plan to implement compression to create more manageable strings. I also need to update the `ChatService` and `ChatComponent` to accommodate different message types and image display. Specifically, I will modify the message structure to support 'text' and 'image' types with appropriate properties. I will create a function to trigger image uploads and to handle file reading to send as a message. **Finalizing the UI** I'm now integrating the image sending feature into the `ChatComponent`'s template. I've added a button to trigger image uploads next to the text input. My logic updates the message display loop to handle different message types - text and image. The plan is to render text directly and display images with an `` tag using the Base64 source. I'm also implementing a "View Image" modal for a full-size display. I decided to prioritize direct Realtime Database storage, given the user's constraints, using a helper to resize. **Implementing Image Compression** I'm now focusing on image handling and database storage optimizations. My plan involves using client-side compression. I've decided on JPEG with 60% quality. I've drafted updates to the `chat.service.ts` and `chat.component.ts`. I also made detailed code modifications, including adding a 'type' property and the 'imageUrl' property to the `ChatMessage` interface, and updating the `sendMessage` function. I will add an image upload button and logic for handling the file input. **Finalizing the Display** I'm now putting the finishing touches on the image display within the chat interface. I've written the Angular template code to display images using the `img` tag when the message type is 'image', which displays messages in the appropriate view. I added the "Lightbox" modal, incorporating image display and the image url. I've designed the class logic for the image-upload button, including `onFileSelected(event)`, `compressImage(file)`, and `viewImage(url)`. I've decided to reuse the existing `ImageIconComponent` and now plan to import it. I also did a mental simulation of the image upload to make sure the flow works as expected: from clicking the upload button to selecting the image, compression to the Base64 string, and finally, uploading and displaying. **Refining the Integration** I'm now zeroing in on the Firebase interaction. My focus is on ensuring the sent image appears correctly in the chat interface as an image, followed by a full-screen view upon click. I'm updating interfaces and send logic in `chat.service.ts` and revising the template and file handling logic in `chat.component.ts`. I also need to position the upload button correctly in the template before the text area.

ဟုတ်ကဲ့၊ Chat မှာ ဓာတ်ပုံပို့လို့ရအောင် ပြုလုပ်ပေးပါမယ်။

Firebase Storage ကို သီးသန့် Setup လုပ်စရာမလိုဘဲ အလုပ်ရှုပ်သက်သာအောင် ပုံတွေကို အရွယ်အစားချုံ့ (Compress) ပြီး Base64 စနစ်နဲ့ ပို့ပေးမယ့်စနစ်ကို ထည့်သွင်းပေးပါမယ်။
